title: Latent tyre-state residual features from a state-space degradation model
summary: Recent F1 tyre-degradation work models latent tyre pace as a hidden state with pit stops acting as resets.
rationale: Recent F1 tyre-degradation work models latent tyre pace as a hidden state with pit stops acting as resets. That is more expressive than raw `TyreLife`, `LapTime_Delta`, or cumulative degradation because it can separate temporary pace noise from true stint decay.
implementation_hint: Fit a lightweight past-only state-space or Kalman-style model per driver-race-stint to estimate latent degradation slope, acceleration, and surprise residuals relative to expected lap-time evolution after controlling for lap count and compound. Use those latent states as features in the existing tabular model.
expected_effect: Could sharpen the trigger signal for 'degradation cliff next lap' cases that static windows miss, especially on compounds/circuits with nonlinear falloff.
risk: Easy to leak if the latent state is smoothed with future laps; use filtering only, never full-sequence smoothing.
